description: Introduces basic French greetings and self-introductions through two friends, Adrienne and Marcel, with audio clips and matching games.
Builds vocabulary for introducing oneself, including name, age, nationality, where you live, and the languages you speak.
Uses a video of two children meeting at the park to practice comprehension of personal information through true/false questions.
Examines a dialogue of two children meeting and teaches interrogative phrases used to ask about name, age, residence, nationality, and languages.
Uses animals to teach French numbers, pairing number words with digits and animal names with their images.
Explores vocabulary for describing animals and their body parts (legs, tail, wings, nest) through images and matching activities.
Introduces the Festival franco-ontarien d'Ottawa through an audio interview, teaching festival-related vocabulary like music, eating, and performers.
Explores food vocabulary found at the festival (poutine, fries, hot dog, popcorn) and how prices are written in French.
Uses a video to teach reading and writing French numbers from 10 to 69, with matching activities pairing words to numerals.
Uses a festival poster to teach calendar and date vocabulary, answering questions about what, when, where, and how much.
Uses a video of a child preparing breakfast to teach household and mealtime vocabulary such as fridge, glass, plate, and milk.
Uses the festival concert schedule to teach telling time in French by matching performers to the hours they sing.
Teaches the days of the week through flashcards with example sentences and audio pronunciation for weekly activities.
Teaches direction words (left, right, west) and how to give and follow directions to places in your community.
Introduces winter activity vocabulary such as cross-country skiing, freestyle skiing, and tobogganing through matching games.
Uses a festival poster to teach seasons, months, and clothing vocabulary by deciding what to wear to the concert.
Teaches colours in French as high-frequency descriptive adjectives using the "Super Mini" video and colour-mixing activities.
Explores vocabulary for describing favourite places (the beach, classroom, park) including colours and animals seen in images.
Uses Madame Bonheur movement videos to teach body part vocabulary (arm, head, leg, body) and how to describe movement.
Uses a Madame Bonheur dance video to reinforce body part vocabulary (foot, arm, hand, head) and complete sentences from the song.
Introduces vegetable vocabulary (zucchini, eggplant, corn, turnip, carrot) and uses a video about giant vegetables with a sorting activity.
Teaches body parts and vegetables through the traditional francophone song "Savez-vous planter les choux" with comprehension questions.
Teaches the polite expressions "s'il vous plaît" and "s'il te plaît" and when to use each, using the "La courtoisie selon Mini" video.
Teaches drink vocabulary (orange juice, milkshake, milk, water) and introduces the concept of a recipe through making sumac lemonade.
